to get started with this is because you're going to get a list. I mean, basically, your list is an asset. And what I mean by that is like, we're talking about online businesses, right? And when you're building a business, I think you need to, you have to focus on building assets in your business. So if you look at it, things like a Google, like getting traffic from Google or getting traffic from other search engines or getting traffic from social media, those are not assets. You know, you could have a number one ranking at Google and get a bunch of traffic. That's not an asset because it can go away at any time. Anytime Google changes an algorithm, it's gone. You could have an affiliate sending you lots of traffic. And that's fantastic, but that's not an asset. ## [09:12 - 12:17]

know, with the product launches, you plan stuff out carefully. So it's not just willy-nilly you're blasting out emails. And actually, I just used the word blast. None of my staff are allowed to use that word. No one can say blast because when you're emailing your list, the key with a list is having a relationship to that list. Having them feel like they know you, like they trust you, like they want to open your emails. Like when your email's coming to them, they know you're not a personal friend sending you that email. But you want them to think of you like a personal friend. You want them to think that they feel like they know you, feel that like these communications are expected and what they get in those communications are what they're looking for. That's really, really important. As we go through this, you'll see, you'll hear me talk about relationship over and over and over again. And it's not a... I don't... Hey, dude, do you want to meet for dinner tonight? You're not sending an email. You're not blasting them, right? You don't blast your friends with a note. I'm pretty friendly with the people working with me, working for me. But I don't have any patience with using that word blast. So the key point isn't... It's not about the word. It's about the relationship. And that's what you need to strive for with your list, that you have a relationship with that list. But the size of your list is not

## [12:17 - 15:08]

important. What's important is whether they open your emails and respond to what you're asking them to do. Whether it's buy something, whether it's click on something, whether it's to go somewhere and opt in, it's to make a comment, it's to give you feedback, whatever it is, whatever influence, whatever action you want to influence. Whether you're making that, having that influence, that's the important part. It's not about having 100,000 people, it's about having an X number of people that open your emails and read your emails. And that's why we focus on relationship. I had a list one time of 300 people. 300 people and I asked them to spend some money with me. And we had, I think 297 of those people basically said yes and spent money with me. It was a $100 purchase. 297 out of 300. That's a relationship with a list. Now, it's insane. Don't ever expect to get anything like that. There's all kinds of extenuating circumstances. It was a very rich community. I'd been helping them out for a long time. It was all about their community with each other and also about this warm relationship with me. But that's like off the charts. I mean, most of the time, if you can get a 5% or 10% response, it's amazing. I got that 99% response. What I'm saying, I'm not trying to brag because it was very extenuating circumstances. It only happened once for me. But that just goes to show you. I mean, that list, I mean, if you do the math, that list generated $29,700. That's an insane number out of a list of 300 people. Much better to have that list than to have 100,000 people where almost no one bought the product. No one opened the emails. So the thing that I want you to remember is that it's not about having that huge list. It's about having a list with a warm relationship. I mean, ideally, the best is you do have a huge list and you have a warm relationship with them. But it's really, it's about results. What kind of results can you get from your list?
